summary: Added a repository-root `.clang-format` file defining the project's clang-format defaults. The configuration is based on LLVM but customizes indentation, pointer alignment, brace wrapping, C-style cast spacing, namespace handling, and disables column limits. The PR body notes that the file format was chosen for Visual Studio Code compatibility and may need adjustment if normal YAML support is required elsewhere.
what_changed: Set `BasedOnStyle: LLVM` as the baseline style.
Disabled derived pointer alignment and forced `PointerAlignment: Left`.
Configured spaces instead of tabs with `IndentWidth: 4` and `TabWidth: 4`.
Enabled custom brace wrapping with `BraceWrapping: { AfterFunction: true }`.
Disabled single-line short if statements via `AllowShortIfStatementsOnASingleLine: false`.
Disabled case label indentation with `IndentCaseLabels: false`.
Disabled automatic line wrapping by setting `ColumnLimit: 0`.
Set C++-oriented options including `AccessModifierOffset: -4`, `NamespaceIndentation: All`, and `FixNamespaceComments: false`.
Configured C-style cast spacing with `SpacesInCStyleCastParentheses: False` and `SpaceAfterCStyleCast: True`.
smart_moves: Centralized formatting rules in a root-level `.clang-format`, making future formatting behavior explicit instead of editor-dependent.
Used LLVM as a known baseline while overriding project-specific preferences, reducing the amount of custom style definition needed.
Set `ColumnLimit: 0`, which is useful in decompilation work where preserving line structure or avoiding churn can matter more than automatic wrapping.
Called out in the PR body that the file uses an idiosyncratic Visual Studio Code-oriented format, flagging a possible compatibility concern instead of assuming all tooling accepts it.
decomp_lessons: Repository-wide formatting configuration helps reduce incidental style churn in future decomp PRs.
For decompilation projects, formatter settings should be introduced carefully because aggressive wrapping or alignment can obscure matching-relevant code structure or create noisy diffs.
If editor-specific formatter syntax is used, document the compatibility tradeoff and be prepared to support standard YAML if contributors' tools require it.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching tactic was present; this PR is formatting/tooling configuration only.
`ColumnLimit: 0` may indirectly support matching work by avoiding formatter-induced line wrapping churn, but the PR does not explicitly state a matching rationale.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modified `.clang-format` or converted it to normal YAML.
Search for formatting-only PRs after PR 484 to see whether this configuration reduced style review churn.
Check whether contributors reported clang-format compatibility issues with Visual Studio Code or other editors.
